problem: A 1.00 g sample of which compound will produce the greatest amount of carbon dioxide after complete combustion with excess oxygen?
How many chlorine atoms are in 1.00 mL of CCl4 (M = 153.8, density = 1.59 g mL^-1)?
What is the molality of Na+ in a solution containing 3.00 g NaCl (M = 58.4), 9.00 g glucose (M = 180.0), and 168 g H2O (M = 18.0)?

25.0 mL of aqueous 0.20 M HCl, 10.0 mL of 0.25 M aqueous HNO3, and 10.0 mL of aqueous 0.30 M Ba(OH)2 are mixed to give a homogeneous solution. In which are the final concentrations of species ranked in order from highest to lowest?

Acetanilide (C6H5NHCOCH3, M = 135.2) can be prepared from aniline (C6H5NH2, M = 93.1) and acetic anhydride ([CH3CO]2O, M = 102.1) according to the balanced equation below.

C6H5NH2 + (CH3CO)2O -> C6H5NHCOCH3 + CH3COOH

If 10.0 g acetanilide is isolated from the reaction of 10.0 g aniline with 10.0 g acetic anhydride, what is the percent yield of the reaction?
10.0 g of a metal chloride salt is dissolved in 90.0 g H2O. This solution has a vapor pressure that is 8.6% lower than that of pure water at the same temperature. What is the identity of the metal?
What is the main purpose of adding boiling chips to a solution that will be heated?
choices: To reduce the boiling point of the solution
To increase the boiling point of the solution
To prevent the solution from superheating
To decrease the time required for the solution to reach its boiling point

problem: An aqueous solution contains one or more of the metal cations Mg2+, Ba2+, and Pb2+ at concentrations of 0.1 M. Addition of an equal volume of 0.1 M sodium sulfate causes formation of a precipitate. Which inference regarding the original solution is correct?
choices: The solution must contain Mg2+ or Ba2+.
The solution must contain Mg2+ or Pb2+.
The solution must contain Ba2+ or Pb2+.
The solution must contain Mg2+, Ba2+, and Pb2+.

problem: Iron(III) nitrate is dissolved in water to form a 0.1 M Fe3+ solution and allowed to stand at room temperature for three days. During that time, a precipitate forms. Addition of which reagent will best prevent formation of a precipitate?
Adding hydrogen peroxide to which reagent will NOT result in gas evolution?

Acetylsalicylic acid is synthesized from salicylic acid, then isolated and dried. The melting point of the product is determined. If some salicylic acid is present, what will be observed? The melting point of pure acetylsalicylic acid is 136 °C and the melting point of pure salicylic acid is 159 °C.
choices: Most of the product will melt at 136 °C, with some flecks of solid remaining until the temperature reaches 159 °C, at which point the remaining material will melt.
The product will melt between 136 and 159 °C.

problem:
A 10.00 g sample of an unknown metal is reacted with hydrochloric acid to form the divalent metal ion and hydrogen gas, which is then collected over water. The molar mass of the metal is calculated to determine the identity of the metal. Which will cause the calculated molar mass to be too large?

I. Not correcting for the vapor pressure of water
II. Not allowing the reaction to go to completion

A room temperature sample of xenon gas in a metal container with a piston has a volume of 1.00 L and a pressure of 4.0 atm. The piston is moved to change the volume of the container to 2.00 L, after which the pressure reads 1.75 atm. Which is the best explanation for this observation?
choices: The volume is miscalibrated and the actual final volume is 1.75 L.
Some xenon has leaked out through the seal in the piston.
The friction of moving the piston has caused the temperature of the gas to increase.
The system is correctly calibrated, well sealed, and remains at room temperature.

problem: In which are the root mean square velocities of Xe(g), Ne(g), and N2(g) at 290 K and 3.0 atm ranked from lowest to highest?

In a sample of pure silane (SiH4), which intermolecular forces are present?

I. London dispersion forces
II. Hydrogen bonding
The melting point of an organic compound X is 5.0 °C. How do the vapor pressures of X(s) and X(l) compare at 10.0 °C?
choices: The vapor pressure of X(s) is less than the vapor pressure of X(l).
The vapor pressure of X(s) is equal to the vapor pressure of X(l).
The vapor pressure of X(s) is greater than the vapor pressure of X(l).
The relative vapor pressures cannot be determined without knowledge of the relative densities of X(s) and X(l).

problem: The arrangement of atoms (represented as black dots) in a pure substance contained in a beaker is shown schematically below. Which is the best description of the phase of the substance?
Which statement about a sample of ammonia (NH3) at its triple point is correct?
choices: It has a pressure of 1.0 atm.
It can be at only one possible temperature.
There are equal amounts of NH3 in the solid, liquid, and gas phases.
If it is in a closed, well-insulated container, it will remain at the triple point when the volume of the container is decreased by a factor of two.

problem:
Given the standard enthalpies of the gas-phase reactions of hydrogen sulfide, what is the standard enthalpy of reaction for the gas-phase combustion of carbon disulfide?

Reaction | DeltaH°rxn, kJ mol^-1
H2S + 1.5 O2 -> SO2 + H2O | -518.2
2 H2S + CO2 -> CS2 + 2 H2O | 67.8
CS2 + 3 O2 -> CO2 + 2 SO2 | ???
Which substance has a standard enthalpy of formation, DeltaH°f, that is greater than zero?

For the reaction

N2(g) + 3 H2(g) -> 2 NH3(g)

DeltaG_rxn = -33 kJ mol^-1 when all gases have a partial pressure of 1.0 bar. Changing the pressure of which gas to 2.0 bar will give the most negative value of DeltaG_rxn?
choices: The value of DeltaG_rxn will be the same regardless of the gas pressures.

problem: Gallium metal melts at 29.8 °C with DeltaH°fusion = 5.59 kJ mol^-1. If equal masses of solid gallium initially at 20.0 °C and liquid gallium initially at 35.0 °C are mixed in a well-insulated container, what percentage of the gallium will be solid after the mixture has reached equilibrium? The molar heat capacity of solid gallium is 26.2 J mol^-1 K^-1 and the molar heat capacity of liquid gallium is 28.0 J mol^-1 K^-1.

cis-2-butene isomerizes to trans-2-butene with DeltaG°rxn = -3.0 kJ mol^-1 at 298 K.

DeltaG°rxn = -3.0 kJ mol^-1

Some cis-2-butene is introduced into a vessel maintained at 298 K, along with a catalyst for isomerization. After equilibrium is attained, what is the mole fraction of cis-2-butene in the vessel?

A certain volume of NaOH(aq) is titrated against HCl(aq) in a well-insulated vessel with constant stirring. Each solution is initially at 22.0 °C, and the temperature of the reaction mixture is monitored after each 2.0 mL of hydrochloric acid is added.

The data in closed circles correspond to an experiment conducted with 20.0 mL NaOH solution and 1.0 M HCl. The open circles were carried out using the same concentration but a different volume of NaOH solution, and using a different concentration of aqueous HCl. Which volume of NaOH and concentration of HCl correspond to the conditions that give the open circles?

Closed circles: Volume NaOH = 20 mL, Concentration HCl = 1.0 M

Hydrogen and nitrogen react according to the following equation:

N2(g) + 3 H2(g) -> 2 NH3(g)

If the concentration of H2(g) is changing at a rate of -12 mol L^-1 s^-1, at what rate is the concentration of NH3(g) changing?
An irreversible reaction R -> P has the rate law Rate = k[R]^2. Which graph will be a straight line?
choices: ln([R]) as a function of t

problem:
For the reaction

O2(g) + 4 HBr(g) -> 2 H2O(g) + Br2(g)

the following mechanism is proposed:

O2(g) + HBr(g) <=> HOOBr(g)    fast, unfavorable
HOOBr(g) + HBr(g) -> 2 HOBr(g)    slow
HOBr(g) + HBr(g) -> H2O(g) + Br2(g)    fast

What rate law is predicted by this mechanism?
The radioisotope 99mTc undergoes gamma decay with a rate constant k = 0.116 h^-1. How much time is required for its activity to decrease to 10.0% of its original activity?
A cotton ball burns more brightly in a jar of 80% O2 than in the air, which is 20% O2. Which is the best explanation for this observation?
choices: The cotton collides more frequently with the O2 inside the jar.
The excited state of O2 is formed more efficiently inside the jar.
The temperature is higher inside the jar.
The jar acts as a catalyst for the reaction.

problem: A reaction has an activation energy of 75.0 kJ mol^-1. At what temperature will the rate constant be twice as large as it is at 298 K?
Lead(II) fluoride is sparingly soluble (Ksp = 4.0 x 10^-8). Which, when added to a saturated solution of PbF2, would increase its solubility to the greatest extent?
The pH of a 0.045 M solution of the monoprotic acid boric acid is 5.30. What is the Ka of boric acid?

NOBr decomposes to NO and Br2 gas in a closed container as shown.

2 NOBr(g) <=> 2 NO(g) + Br2(g)    DeltaH°rxn = +16.3 kJ mol^-1

Which changes will result in an increase in the number of moles of NO present at equilibrium?

I. Increasing the temperature
II. Decreasing the volume of the container

At a certain temperature, SO2Cl2 decomposes into SO2 and Cl2 as shown below.

SO2Cl2(g) <=> SO2(g) + Cl2(g)    Kp = 0.50

An equal number of moles of SO2Cl2, SO2, and Ar are introduced into a sealed flask to give a total pressure of 1.80 bar. The flask is maintained at the same temperature until equilibrium is attained. What is the final pressure of Cl2(g)?

A buffer solution consists of 0.100 mol Na3PO4 and 0.100 mol Na2HPO4 dissolved in water. How much of which component of the buffer must be added to change the pH to 12.25? The pKa of HPO4^2- is 12.37.

A 0.100 M aqueous solution of H2SeO3 is titrated with 1.000 M NaOH solution. At the point marked with a circle on the titration curve, which species comprise at least 10% of the total selenium in solution?
What is the average oxidation state of sulfur in calcium tetrathionate, CaS4O6?

When the equation for the oxidation of Cu(s) by NO3-(aq) to give Cu2+(aq) and NO(g) in acidic aqueous solution is balanced using smallest whole number coefficients, what is the coefficient for NO3-(aq)?
Water is electrolyzed with a current of 10.0 A until 132 L of hydrogen gas (after drying, measured at STP) has been produced. How much time does this take?
A 1.0 M solution of Cu(NO3)2 is electrolyzed using the setup shown below. What reaction occurs at the cathode?

What is E° for the oxidation of mercury(I) ion by silver ion?

2 Ag+(aq) + Hg2^2+(aq) -> 2 Ag(s) + 2 Hg2+(aq)

Half-reaction | E°, V
Ag+(aq) + e- -> Ag(s) | +0.80
2 Hg2+(aq) + 2e- -> Hg2^2+(aq) | +0.91

What is the standard reduction potential of PbF2(s) at 298 K? The Ksp of PbF2 at 298 K is 4.0 x 10^-8.

Half-reaction | E°, V
Pb2+(aq) + 2e- -> Pb(s) | -0.13
PbF2(s) + 2e- -> Pb(s) + 2 F-(aq) | ???
Which species has the largest ionic radius?
Which of the following statements regarding ionization energy of gas-phase atoms is correct?
choices: The first ionization energy of a group 1 element is always greater than the first ionization energy of the group 2 element in the same row of the periodic table.
In the second row of the periodic table, the first ionization energy is directly proportional to the atomic radius.
Among the elements from Al (Z = 13) to Ar (Z = 18), the first ionization energy increases monotonically with atomic number.
Among the elements of group 16, the first ionization energy decreases monotonically with atomic number.

problem: How many electrons in a ground state gas-phase Al atom have the quantum number l = 1?
What is the ground-state electron configuration of a gas-phase Mn2+ ion?
What wavelengths of light are capable of breaking a bond with a bond strength of 425 kJ mol^-1?
When 105Te decays by alpha emission, how many protons are in the daughter nuclide that is produced?
Which species has the largest O-N-O angle?
Which molecule has the shortest bond?

Which molecules are described by molecular orbital theory as having a bond order of 2.0?

I. CO
II. NO
Which is the best description of the geometry of IF7?
Carbon monoxide, CO, has a very small dipole moment. Which is the best explanation for this observation?
choices: The carbon-oxygen pi bonds are polarized in the opposite direction from the carbon-oxygen sigma bonds, so the bond dipoles tend to cancel each other out.
Carbon and oxygen each have the same number of lone pairs despite the fact that carbon has fewer valence electrons than oxygen, partially canceling the bond dipoles.
Neither carbon nor oxygen has a formal charge in the Lewis structure of CO, giving an electron distribution that is minimally asymmetric.
Carbon is sp-hybridized in CO, making it more electronegative than a typical carbon atom.

problem: Which species has the smallest tendency to dimerize?
How many stable one-carbon molecules contain both hydrogen and chlorine and no other elements?
Which property best accounts for the fact that acetic acid (CH3COOH) is far more acidic than ethanol (CH3CH2OH) in aqueous solution?
choices: Acetic acid is more soluble in water than ethanol.
Acetic acid has two hydrogen bond acceptors that can interact with water, while ethanol has only one.
Acetic acid's conjugate base has its negative charge delocalized over two oxygens, while ethanol's conjugate base has its negative charge localized on one oxygen.
Acetic acid can form a hydrate CH3C(OH)3 in water, while ethanol cannot react with water in this way.

problem:
Which isomers of xylene would produce only a single mononitration product on treatment with HNO3/H2SO4?

I. ortho-xylene
II. para-xylene

Under certain conditions, HBr adds to 2-methylpropene to give predominantly 2-bromo-2-methylpropane rather than isomeric 1-bromo-2-methylpropane. Which is the best mechanistic explanation for this regioselectivity?

H2C=C(CH3)2 + HBr -> CH3-C(Br)(CH3)-CH3
choices: H+ adds preferentially to C-1 to form the more stable carbocation.
Br- adds preferentially to C-2 to form the more stable carbanion.
H* adds preferentially to C-1 to form the more stable radical.
Br* adds preferentially to C-2 to form the more stable radical.

problem: Which will most reliably distinguish between an alcohol and an ether with the formula C4H10O?
choices: The alcohol will have the higher normal boiling point.
The alcohol will rapidly decolorize bromine while the ether will not.
The alcohol will absorb visible light while the ether will not.
The alcohol will form an electrically conductive aqueous solution while the ether will not.

problem: Which form of the amino acid alanine is most abundant in aqueous solution at pH = 7.0?
